## Analysis: Rudel CLI (`apps/cli/`)

### Overview

The Rudel CLI is a Bun-based command-line tool (`rudel` on npm) built with `@stricli/core` for command routing and argument parsing. It has 8 user-facing commands (one hidden) for authenticating with the Rudel API, uploading Claude Code session transcripts, and managing auto-upload hooks. The CLI uses two distinct output paradigms: `@clack/prompts` in exactly one command (`upload`), and raw `process.stdout.write` / `node:readline` everywhere else.

### Command Framework

The CLI uses `@stricli/core` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/package.json:43`) for command definition and routing.

**Entry point**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/bin/cli.ts:1-5`
- Imports the `app` from `app.ts` and calls `run(app, process.argv.slice(2), { process })`

**Route map**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/app.ts:12-27`
- Registers 7 top-level routes: `login`, `logout`, `whoami`, `upload`, `enable`, `disable`, `set-org`
- Registers 1 nested route map: `hooks` (hidden from help via `hideRoute: { hooks: true }` at line 25)
- The `hooks` route has subcommand `claude` which has subcommand `session-end`, giving the full path: `rudel hooks claude session-end`

### Complete Command Inventory

#### 1. `rudel login` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/login.ts`)

**Output method**: `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` (lines 13-14)
**User interaction**: None (browser-based OAuth flow, no terminal prompts)

Starts a local HTTP server on a random port (line 31-65), constructs a login URL with `cli_callback` and `state` query params (line 68), opens the user's browser (lines 74-82), and waits up to 120 seconds for a callback containing a token (lines 85-101). Validates the token against `/rpc/me` (lines 106-118), then saves credentials via `saveCredentials()` (line 125).

**Flags**: `--api-base` (default `https://app.rudel.ai`), `--web-url` (default `https://app.rudel.ai`)

#### 2. `rudel logout` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/logout.ts`)

**Output method**: `process.stdout.write` (line 5)
**User interaction**: None

Loads credentials and if present, clears them via `clearCredentials()` (line 13).

#### 3. `rudel whoami` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/whoami.ts`)

**Output method**: `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` (lines 5-6)
**User interaction**: None

Calls `verifyAuth()` which loads credentials and pings the API via `client.me()`. Displays user name and email on success.

#### 4. `rudel upload [session]` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts`)

**Output method**: Mixed -- `@clack/prompts` for interactive mode (line 1: `import * as p from "@clack/prompts"`), `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` for single-session mode (lines 165-169)
**User interaction**: `p.multiselect()` for project selection in interactive mode (lines 93-98)

Two code paths determined at `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts:275-283`:
- **No args** (`sessions.length === 0`): Calls `runInteractiveUpload()` which uses `@clack/prompts` throughout -- `p.intro()`, `p.spinner()`, `p.multiselect()`, `p.log.*()`, `p.outro()`, `p.cancel()`, `p.isCancel()`
- **With arg**: Calls `runSingleUpload()` which uses raw `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` exclusively

**Flags**: `--tag` (enum), `--endpoint`, `--classify` / `-c`, `--dry-run` / `-n`, `--org` / `-o`

#### 5. `rudel enable` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts`)

**Output method**: `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` (lines 40-41)
**User interaction**: `node:readline` `createInterface` for two prompts: `promptChoice()` (line 14-27) and `promptYesNo()` (line 29-37)

This is the most complex interactive command. Full flow documented below.

#### 6. `rudel disable` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/disable.ts`)

**Output method**: `process.stdout.write` (line 5)
**User interaction**: None

Checks if the hook is installed via `agent.isHookInstalled()`, and if so, calls `agent.removeHook()`.

#### 7. `rudel set-org` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/set-org.ts`)

**Output method**: `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` (lines 23-24)
**User interaction**: `node:readline` `createInterface` for `promptChoice()` (lines 7-20)

Similar org selection flow as `enable`, but standalone. Loads credentials, fetches orgs, shows numbered list, uses readline to get choice, saves via `setProjectOrgId()`.

#### 8. `rudel hooks claude session-end` (hidden) (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts`)

**Output method**: None (completely silent)
**User interaction**: None (reads JSON from stdin)

This is the hook handler invoked by Claude Code after a session ends. Reads `{ session_id, transcript_path, cwd }` from stdin (line 29-32), loads credentials, reads the transcript, extracts subagents, gets git info, resolves the org ID, and uploads. All errors are swallowed silently (line 65-67) because this runs as a background async process.

---

### How `rudel enable` Works

**File**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts`

#### Step 1: Authentication (lines 44-49)
Calls `verifyAuth()` from `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/auth.ts:22-60`, which loads stored credentials from `~/.rudel/credentials.json` and pings the API via `client.me()`. If not authenticated, writes error and exits with code 1.

#### Step 2: Fetch Organizations (lines 54-62)
Creates an oRPC client via `createApiClient(credentials)` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/api-client.ts:11-21`) and calls `client.listMyOrganizations()`. This hits the shared contract defined in `@rudel/api-routes`.

#### Step 3: Organization Selection (lines 64-101)
Three branches:

1. **Zero orgs** (line 64-70): Error message, exit code 1.

2. **Exactly one org** (lines 82-84): Auto-selects it, prints `Using organization: {name}`.

3. **Multiple orgs, existing config matches** (lines 85-87): If the project already has an org configured (checked via `getProjectOrgId(cwd)` from `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/project-config.ts:68-73`) and that org is still in the user's org list, it prints `Currently configured for: {name}` and keeps the existing selection.

4. **Multiple orgs, no existing match** (lines 88-101): Prints a numbered list and calls `promptChoice()` which uses `node:readline`'s `createInterface()` (line 14-27). The `promptChoice()` function creates a readline interface, asks the question, parses the number, and defaults to `1` if input is invalid (NaN, out of range, or empty).

#### Step 4: Save Project Config (line 103)
Calls `setProjectOrgId(cwd, selectedOrgId)` from `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/project-config.ts:76-84`. This:
- Derives a project key via `getProjectKey(cwd)` (lines 39-66) -- prefers git remote URL (normalized), falls back to git root path, then falls back to raw cwd
- Loads `~/.rudel/projects.json`
- Saves `{ organizationId }` under the project key
- Writes back with mode `0o600`

#### Step 5: Install Hook (lines 106-113)
Gets the default agent via `getDefaultAgent()` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/agents/index.ts:6-8`) which returns a `ClaudeCodeAgent` instance.

`ClaudeCodeAgent.installHook()` delegates to `addHook()` in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/claude-settings.ts:77-97`:
- Finds the `.claude` directory by walking up from cwd looking for an existing `.claude` dir, falling back to git root, falling back to cwd (lines 26-48)
- Reads `.claude/settings.json`
- Adds a `SessionEnd` hook entry: `{ matcher: "", hooks: [{ type: "command", command: "rudel hooks claude session-end", async: true }] }`
- Writes the updated settings back

If the hook is already installed, it prints "Auto-upload hook is already enabled. Organization updated." (line 109).

#### Step 6: Upload Existing Sessions (lines 116-164)
Calls `agent.findProjectSessions(cwd)` on `ClaudeCodeAgent` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/agents/claude-code.ts:42-51`) which:
- Encodes the project path (replaces `/` with `-`)
- Looks in `~/.claude/projects/{encoded}/` for `.jsonl` files (excluding `agent-*` files)
- If not found by encoding, falls back to iterating all project dirs and decoding each one via `decodeProjectPath()` to find a match

If sessions are found, calls `promptYesNo()` (enable.ts lines 29-37) using `node:readline` -- asks `Found {N} previous session(s). Upload them now? [y/N]`. Only proceeds if user types exactly `y` (case-insensitive).

Then iterates each session (lines 126-164), reading transcript, extracting subagent IDs, reading subagent files, getting git info, and uploading via `uploadSession()` from `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts:15-34` which creates an oRPC client and calls `client.ingestSession(request)`.

---

### UX Library Usage Summary

#### `@clack/prompts` usage

**Only used in one file**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts:1` (`import * as p from "@clack/prompts"`)

**Only used in one code path**: `runInteractiveUpload()` (lines 33-155), which is the no-argument path.

Features used from `@clack/prompts`:
- `p.intro()` -- line 41
- `p.spinner()` -- lines 43, 124
- `p.multiselect()` -- lines 93-98
- `p.isCancel()` -- line 100
- `p.cancel()` -- line 101
- `p.log.info()` -- line 109
- `p.log.success()` -- line 134
- `p.log.error()` -- lines 36, 137
- `p.log.warn()` -- lines 49, 139, 142
- `p.outro()` -- lines 50, 147, 149

#### `node:readline` usage (raw prompts)

Used in two files:

1. **`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts`**:
   - `import { createInterface } from "node:readline"` (line 2)
   - `promptChoice()` function (lines 14-27) -- numbered selection
   - `promptYesNo()` function (lines 29-37) -- yes/no confirmation

2. **`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/set-org.ts`**:
   - `import { createInterface } from "node:readline"` (line 1)
   - `promptChoice()` function (lines 7-20) -- numbered selection, duplicate of the one in `enable.ts`

#### `process.stdout.write` / `process.stderr.write` usage (raw output)

Used in every command file except `hooks/claude/session-end.ts`:
- `login.ts` lines 13-14
- `logout.ts` line 5
- `whoami.ts` lines 5-6
- `upload.ts` lines 165-169 (single-session mode only)
- `enable.ts` lines 40-41
- `disable.ts` line 5
- `set-org.ts` lines 23-24

All follow the same pattern: `const write = (msg: string) => process.stdout.write(\`${msg}\n\`)`.

---

### Commands Using Raw I/O for Interactive Prompts (non-clack)

| Command | File | Interaction Type | Lines |
|---------|------|-----------------|-------|
| `enable` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` | `node:readline` numbered choice | 14-27 |
| `enable` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` | `node:readline` yes/no confirm | 29-37 |
| `set-org` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/set-org.ts` | `node:readline` numbered choice | 7-20 |

### Commands Using Raw I/O for Output Only (no interaction, just display)

| Command | File | Lines |
|---------|------|-------|
| `login` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/login.ts` | 13-14 |
| `logout` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/logout.ts` | 5 |
| `whoami` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/whoami.ts` | 5-6 |
| `disable` | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/disable.ts` | 5 |
| `upload` (single-session path) | `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts` | 165-169 |

---

### Key Patterns

- **Lazy command loading**: Every command uses Stricli's `loader: async () => ({ default: fn })` pattern, meaning the command handler function is only loaded when the command is actually invoked.
- **Agent abstraction**: The `CodingAgent` interface (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/agents/types.ts`) abstracts agent-specific operations (hook install/remove, session discovery). Currently only `ClaudeCodeAgent` implements it.
- **Duplicated `promptChoice()`**: The `promptChoice()` function is identically defined in both `enable.ts` (lines 14-27) and `set-org.ts` (lines 7-20) -- same signature, same logic, same readline approach.
- **Two UX paradigms**: Interactive `upload` uses `@clack/prompts` with its styled spinner, multiselect, and logging. All other commands use bare `process.stdout.write` with a `write()` helper and `node:readline` for input.
- **Silent hook handler**: `session-end.ts` produces no output at all and swallows all errors, since it runs as an async background process triggered by Claude Code.

#### Step 6: Upload Existing Sessions (lines 116-164)
Calls `agent.findProjectSessions(cwd)` on `ClaudeCodeAgent` (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/agents/claude-code.ts:42-51`) which:
- Encodes the project path (replaces `/` with `-`)
- Looks in `~/.claude/projects/{encoded}/` for `.jsonl` files (excluding `agent-*` files)
- If not found by encoding, falls back to iterating all project dirs and decoding each one via `decodeProjectPath()` to find a match

If sessions are found, calls `promptYesNo()` (enable.ts lines 29-37) using `node:readline` -- asks `Found {N} previous session(s). Upload them now? [y/N]`. Only proceeds if user types exactly `y` (case-insensitive).

### Key Patterns

- **Lazy command loading**: Every command uses Stricli's `loader: async () => ({ default: fn })` pattern, meaning the command handler function is only loaded when the command is actually invoked.
- **Agent abstraction**: The `CodingAgent` interface (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/lib/agents/types.ts`) abstracts agent-specific operations (hook install/remove, session discovery). Currently only `ClaudeCodeAgent` implements it.

- **Silent hook handler**: `session-end.ts` produces no output at all and swallows all errors, since it runs as an async background process triggered by Claude Code.

Here's a summary of all the changes:

**`enable.ts`** - The main ask. Replaced `node:readline` `promptChoice()` and `promptYesNo()` with:
- `p.select()` for org selection - users now get an arrow-key navigable list with org slugs as hints instead of a raw numbered list
- `p.confirm()` for the "upload existing sessions?" prompt instead of raw readline y/N
- `p.intro/outro` to frame the command, `p.spinner` for the upload progress, `p.log.*` for all messaging

**`set-org.ts`** - Same treatment: replaced the duplicated `promptChoice()` readline with `p.select()`, with the current org marked via hint. Added `initialValue` so the cursor starts on the current selection.

**`login.ts`** - Replaced raw `process.stdout.write` with `p.intro`, a `p.spinner` that runs while waiting for the browser callback (much better than a static "Opening browser..." message), and `p.log.*` for success/error states.

**`logout.ts`, `whoami.ts`, `disable.ts`** - Switched from raw `process.stdout.write` to `p.log.*` for consistent styled output across the entire CLI.

**`app.ts`** - Fixed a pre-existing type-check error from PR #73 (missing `with { type: "json" }` on the package.json import).

content.thinking:
Great news - `@clack/prompts` has exactly what we need. There are three great options:

1. **`p.tasks()`** - A sequential task runner that shows checkmarks as items complete, with the ability to update status text per-task
2. **`p.progress()`** - A progress bar that extends spinner with `advance()` 
3. **`p.taskLog()`** - A streaming log display for long-running operations

For the upload of previous sessions in `enable.ts`, `p.tasks()` is perfect - each session upload becomes a task with its own spinner/checkmark. Let me implement this.


Updating enable.ts to use `p.tasks()` for the session upload section...

content.text:
`@clack/prompts` has exactly what we need - no need for another framework. It offers:

- **`p.tasks()`** - sequential task runner showing spinner per task, checkmarks on completion
- **`p.progress()`** - actual progress bar with `advance()` and visual styles (`light`/`heavy`/`block`)
- **`p.taskLog()`** - streaming log display with groups and rolling output

For the session upload, `p.tasks()` is the best fit - each session gets its own spinner while uploading and a checkmark/error when done. Let me update `enable.ts`.

content.content: The file /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/stuttgart/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts has been updated successfully.

content.text:
All 11 tasks pass. The upload section in `enable.ts` now uses `p.tasks()` - each session gets its own line with:
- A spinner while it's being processed (showing "Reading transcript...", "Resolving git info...", "Uploading...")
- A checkmark with "Uploaded" on success, or the error message on failure

This is much better than the previous single spinner with a counter update. No new framework needed - `@clack/prompts` had everything built in.

refactor: modernize CLI UX with @clack/prompts across all commands

Replace raw process.stdout.write and node:readline prompts with @clack/prompts for consistent, styled terminal UI throughout the CLI.

- enable: Use p.select() for org selection with hints, p.confirm() for upload prompt, p.tasks() for per-session upload progress with status messages
- set-org: Use p.select() with initial value and current org indicator
- login: Show p.spinner() while waiting for browser auth callback
- logout, whoami, disable: Switch to p.log.* for styled output
- All commands: Consistent use of p.intro/outro, p.log.*, removing duplicated readline helpers

Also fix TypeScript error in app.ts: add `with { type: "json" }` to package.json import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
